name: Infected Broodmother

attacks.name: Basic Bite
attacks.tell: She lowers her head and throws out a short frontal bite, almost identical to the normal Broodmother's.
attacks.howToAvoid: Block it or step off the line as soon as she closes the gap.
attacks.notes: It only exists in the first life. The PDF explicitly states that it disappears from phase 2 onward.

attacks.name: 3-Hit Combo Bite
attacks.tell: She chains three quick bites with almost no pause between them.
attacks.howToAvoid: Block all three timings or roll out before the sequence finishes.
attacks.notes: This is also exclusive to the first life; she loses it when phase 2 starts.

attacks.name: 5-Hit Combo Bite
attacks.tell: She stays on top of the player and extends the sequence into five linked bites.
attacks.howToAvoid: Do not swing back too early; block until the real end or disengage from the chain.
attacks.notes: It appears from phase 2 onward, and in phase 3 it gains slow, medium, and fast variants chosen at random.

attacks.name: Charged Pounce
attacks.tell: She tucks in for a moment and then launches her full body forward.
attacks.howToAvoid: Dodge sideways at the last moment; backpedaling in a straight line is usually not enough.
attacks.notes: This is the phase 1 power bite and it can apply heavy Venom. She loses it when phase 2 starts.

attacks.name: Dodge
attacks.tell: She hops sideways or backward to reposition before the next punish.
attacks.howToAvoid: Do not chase the animation blindly; wait to see what she links into next.
attacks.notes: In phase 2 the move starts leaving fungal dust, and it can also stagger on a non-perfect block.

attacks.name: Jump Attack
attacks.tell: She leaps high and crashes down on the player, bringing back the normal Broodmother's basic jump pattern.
attacks.howToAvoid: Roll out of the true impact circle; the surrounding fungal burst punishes staying close.
attacks.notes: It appears in phase 2 and, according to the PDF, applies -Exhaustion Recovery for 20 seconds.

attacks.name: Side Lunge
attacks.tell: She dodges to one side of the player and then cuts back in with a very fast bite.
attacks.howToAvoid: Do not trail the first sidestep too late with your camera; the bite comes immediately after.
attacks.notes: In phase 3 it becomes longer, faster, and deadlier, and it can apply weak Venom on hit.

attacks.name: Fungal Scream
attacks.tell: She rises up and unleashes an infected shriek that triggers the bomb rain across the arena.
attacks.howToAvoid: A perfect block denies the worst part of the debuff. Otherwise keep moving and do not stand under multiple bombs.
attacks.notes: According to the PDF, the scream applies -Attack Damage for 30 seconds and -Increased Healing for the rest of the fight unless perfect-blocked.

attacks.name: Fungal Bomb Rain
attacks.tell: After the scream, multiple fungal bombs fall across the arena and detonate out of sync.
attacks.howToAvoid: Run with intent and stay out of the central water while the pattern is falling.
attacks.notes: Each detonation can heal the boss if she is nearby and also applies -Damage Resist to the player for the rest of the fight.

attacks.name: Strange Spore Egg
attacks.tell: She leaves a fungal egg sac on the ground, similar to an Orb Weaver's.
attacks.howToAvoid: Break it in a real opening or leave its radius before the 8-second detonation.
attacks.notes: The PDF says it explodes after 8 seconds, can heal her if she is nearby, and also applies a permanent -Damage Resist penalty for the rest of the fight.

attacks.name: Dodge Dust Cloud
attacks.tell: After the dodge she leaves a fungal dust cloud exactly where she just moved.
attacks.howToAvoid: Do not path through the cloud on reflex; she often chains a lunge or pounce to catch you coming out.
attacks.notes: The PDF states that the cloud applies -Movement Speed while inside it and -Increased Healing for 20 seconds.

attacks.name: Spore Shot
attacks.tell: She spits an explosive spore like the infected wolf spider, detonating on proximity or lingering on the floor if it misses.
attacks.howToAvoid: Do not heal at mid-range and do not panic if it misses: the spore is still a mine until it goes off.
attacks.notes: In phase 3 its explosions apply smaller but stacking -Damage Resist and -Healing Received debuffs until the fight ends.

attacks.name: Infected Gnat Explosion
attacks.tell: During the upgraded scream, 3-5 infected gnats appear in the upper part of the arena.
attacks.howToAvoid: Do not ignore them while reading the boss; they explode shortly after spawning and turn any bad route into death.
attacks.notes: This is not a traditional summon meant for melee cleanup; they are living projectiles tied to the phase 3 scream.

bossPhases.label: Phase 1
bossPhases.summary: The first life uses almost the normal Broodmother's base kit, only with faster movement and the infected water already altering the fight.
bossPhases.trigger: It begins when she climbs down from the infected ceiling and fully wakes up inside the arena.
specialTraits: The infected water in the center already gives her lifesteal, immunity to the -Attack Speed effect, and crit chance while she stays in it.

attacks.name: Basic Bite
attacks.tell: She lowers her head and throws out a short frontal bite, almost identical to the normal Broodmother's.
attacks.howToAvoid: Block it or step off the line as soon as she closes the gap.
attacks.notes: It only exists in the first life. The PDF explicitly states that it disappears from phase 2 onward.

attacks.name: 3-Hit Combo Bite
attacks.tell: She chains three quick bites with almost no pause between them.
attacks.howToAvoid: Block all three timings or roll out before the sequence finishes.

attacks.name: Charged Pounce
attacks.tell: She tucks in for a moment and then launches her full body forward.
attacks.howToAvoid: Dodge sideways at the last moment; backpedaling in a straight line is usually not enough.
attacks.notes: This is the phase 1 power bite and it can apply heavy Venom. She loses it when phase 2 starts.

attacks.name: Dodge
attacks.tell: She hops sideways or backward to reposition before the next punish.
attacks.howToAvoid: Do not chase the animation blindly; wait to see what she links into next.
attacks.notes: In phase 2 the move starts leaving fungal dust, and it can also stagger on a non-perfect block.

bossPhases.label: Phase 2
bossPhases.summary: After the first revival she drops the basic bite, the 3-hit combo, and the power bite. In exchange she gains more explosive pressure, less downtime, and a much dirtier kit.
bossPhases.trigger: It begins when she empties her first health bar and revives with more health, more stun threshold, and less time between attacks.
newPatterns: From here on the dodge leaves a fungal cloud and the bomb rain becomes a central part of the fight.
specialTraits: She also increases the explosive-damage-to-healing conversion and cuts 1.5 seconds from the downtime between attacks.

attacks.name: 5-Hit Combo Bite
attacks.tell: She stays on top of the player and extends the sequence into five linked bites.
attacks.howToAvoid: Do not swing back too early; block until the real end or disengage from the chain.
attacks.notes: It appears from phase 2 onward, and in phase 3 it gains slow, medium, and fast variants chosen at random.

attacks.notes: The PDF states that the cloud applies -Movement Speed while inside it and -Increased Healing for 20 seconds.

bossPhases.label: Phase 3
bossPhases.summary: The final life pushes the pace even harder, adds 50% attack speed and 25% more damage, and layers on her meanest pressure: explosive spores and infected gnats.
bossPhases.trigger: It begins when she empties her second health bar and revives for the last time with more health, more stun threshold, and even shorter recovery gaps.
newPatterns: The upgraded scream can bring in 3-5 explosive infected gnats, and the 5-hit combo can come out in slow, medium, or very fast variants.
specialTraits: In this phase the healing she gets from explosive damage climbs even higher, making explosive weapons an even worse idea than before.

attacks.name: 5-Hit Combo Bite
attacks.tell: She stays on top of the player and extends the sequence into five linked bites.
attacks.howToAvoid: Do not swing back too early; block until the real end or disengage from the chain.
attacks.notes: It appears from phase 2 onward, and in phase 3 it gains slow, medium, and fast variants chosen at random.

description: An infected and utterly devastating version of the Hedge Broodmother. It is the hardest fight in Grounded and rules the Haze as a source of plague and explosions.
behavior: The fight works like a three-life war of attrition. Every phase revives with more effective health, more aggression, more healing from explosive damage, and a dirtier set of bombs, eggs, dust clouds, and spore shots.
interactionWithPlayer: It is hostile immediately and webs the cave shut. The player is not only fighting the boss, but also the infected water in the middle of the arena, which heavily modifies both her buffs and your debuffs.
interactionWithCreatures: Its infected water and explosions alter anything they touch. The boss does not need permanent minions; the arena itself acts as lethal support.
strategy: Fresh is the only clean damage line and explosives are actively harmful because they heal her. The arena water must also be respected: drinking it or letting her fight inside it multiplies the problems dramatically.
respawnInfo: Repeatable with a Moldy Hoagie after sealing the Haze Canister.

combatStats.attackDamageSummary:
Bite: 90
3-hit combo: 80
5-hit combo: 90
Charged pounce: 145
Dodge: 50
Jump attack: 130
Side lunge: 120
Scream: 39
Fungal bomb rain: 80
Strange spore egg: 175
Gnat explosion: 85
Spore shot: 80

specialTraits: Explosives are worse than useless: they heal her, and that healing grows with each phase.
The water in the center of the arena grants lifesteal and crit to the boss while inflicting brutal side effects on the player.
